title: Bring back the version update bot with GitHub Actions
body:
As of earlier this year, there was a nice robot called "yosys-bot" which, every 24 hours, would run `make bumpversion` on the repository and commit the result, if and only if a commit was made in that time. That is no longer the case, and the bot has been defunct for several months (and, for the sake of both brevity and keeping discussions on topic, I won't further speculate or reason as to why.)

However, I would like this bot to return in some form. I am the maintainer of Yosys for Nixpkgs, and that robot's autotagging was very useful. I would regularly update the latest version of the yosys package with version numbers based on those version bump commits. They provided a very useful "line in the sand" which I could use to push and scope updates around. In fact, our build system specifically checks that the version number of the Nix package and the version in `YOSYS_VER` matches as a sanity check to ensure they're correct. As of now, nixpkgs is using a build from February, ` 0.9+4052`, which was the last version bump that was performed if I'm correct.[1]

So it would be nice if we could bring it back, and since everything these days is consolidating around GitHub Actions, I think it's quite viable to just have a bot that runs every 24 hours and does this. Actually, 24 hours might be too frequent; one week would also be fine (a week gives a little buffer room to find any "obvious" regressions that might sneak in, for instance.)

Of course, the thing is, this kind of work is very boring and annoying and has a few tiny sharp edges and because you can't test GH Actions you normally do this thing where you push to a "testing" branch 400,000 times in order to get all the little bits right. So it's easy, but also annoying at the same time. Alas. But the basic idea is just:

1. Run at some interval
2. Run `make versionbump`
3. Commit the result under some identity

Again, this is useful for me and I haven't seen anyone else complain. So perhaps I'll get around to doing this myself when I feel like it...

---

[1] There is of course another point here which is "why don't you just use the stable release", but in general I find yosys to be extremely stable in general and the features since the 0.9 release are significant; I'm a user of yosys myself and this means that reverting back to a stable release is a serious regression in terms of features and support. And as a user and a package maintainer, this creates a kind of positive feedback loop; as a result I think the yosys package in Nixpkgs is very high quality and easy to use (along with several others I maintain, like `nextpnr`). Finally, users of `nixpkgs` are able to use those packages on any system, so many people use the latest tip of the `master` branch to get very fresh packages, `yosys` included.

(Then, there are also projects like YoWASP that might benefit; but there seems to already be infrastructure in place for doing nightly builds, so the only thing that would change is having a more accurate version number.)

So I think regardless of the situation with a new stable release, the autotagging bot is still quite useful.
